title: Copyright Registration

hero.headline: Safeguard Your Creativity with Copyright Registration
hero.subtext: Protect your original work legally with expert copyright services from Professionals Law.

mainHeading: Copyright Registration Online – Apply Copyright Licence in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: Copyright is a right given by the law to creators of literary, dramatic, musical and artistic works and producers of cinematograph films and sound recordings. Under the Copyright Act, 1957, creators are given sole rights over the reproduction, adaptation and distribution of their work. Copyright does not protect ideas but protects the expression of those ideas. At Professionals Law, we help authors, artists, businesses and content creators secure their intellectual assets through efficient copyright registration. Professionals Law ensures that your work is protected under Indian law and helps you establish legal proof of ownership.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: In modern digital arena, creative works can be easily copied, shared or misused without the consent of its creator. Copyright registration provides a legal shield against such unauthorized use and infringement so that the creator can protect his creative ideas. It acts as prima facie evidence in the court of law and simplifies legal proceedings in the event of discourse, disputes or wrangles. Professionals Law provides comprehensive assistance to ensure that your original work receives due recognition and protection from infringement, piracy and duplication.

criteria: Any person or entity who has created an original literary, dramatic, musical, artistic, cinematographic or software work is eligible for copyright registration, including:

body.title: Legal Protection:
body.description: Registered copyright serves as concrete proof of ownership and allows you to take swift legal action in case of infringement.

body.title: Exclusive Rights:
body.description: It grants you exclusive rights to reproduce, distribute, translate, perform and license the work.

body.title: Economic Benefits:
body.description: Copyright can be monetized through licensing, royalties, assignments and transfers, offering a long-term revenue stream.

body.title: Global Recognition:
body.description: Copyright protection is recognized in most countries through international treaties. Professionals Law helps facilitate this globally.

body.title: Enhances Market Value:
body.description: Owning registered copyrights improves your brand's professional credibility and increases the value of your creative assets.

documents: Name, address and nationality of the applicant
Nature and title of the work
Class and description of the work
Date of creation and publication (if applicable)
Two copies of the work (soft or hard)

body.description: Professionals Law’s experts classify your work into the correct copyright category (literary, artistic, musical, software, etc.)
We draft and file the copyright application online along with the required documents and copies of the work.
Once submitted, a diary number is issued by the Copyright Office for tracking purposes.
A mandatory 30-day period is given for objections by third parties, if any.
If no objection is filed, the application proceeds to examination. In case of objection, a hearing is scheduled.
Upon approval, the Registrar of Copyrights issues a registration certificate, giving legal proof of ownership.

body.title: Examination & Registration Certificate
body.description: 2 to 5 months (depending on objections, if any)

body.title: Infringement Penalties
body.description: Copyright infringement may result in civil and criminal liability, including fines from ₹50,000 to ₹2,00,000 and imprisonment up to 3 years.

body.title: False Representation
body.description: Submitting false claims can attract penalties and potential legal action.

body.title: Unauthorized Use
body.description: Using copyrighted work without permission can lead to injunctions, loss of profits or damage claims.

body.question: What can be copyrighted?
body.answer: Literary works, musical compositions, films, sound recordings, software, artworks, scripts and photographs, among others.

body.question: Can unpublished work be copyrighted?
body.answer: Yes, copyright protects both published and unpublished works.

body.question: How long is copyright valid?
body.answer: Generally, copyright lasts for the lifetime of the author plus 60 years after their death.

body.question: Can I copyright my software code?
body.answer: Yes, software (source code and object code) is considered a literary work under copyright law.

body.question: What’s the difference between copyright and trademark?
body.answer: Copyright protects creative expressions, while trademark protects brand identity like logos and names.

body.question: Is copyright registration mandatory?
body.answer: While not mandatory, registration provides legal proof and simplifies enforcement during infringement cases.

body.question: Can two people hold a joint copyright?
body.answer: Yes, joint authorship is allowed and both parties hold equal rights unless otherwise agreed.

body.question: What happens if someone copies my work?
body.answer: With a registered copyright, you can issue a cease & desist notice or file a suit for infringement. Professionals Law can assist in both.

body.question: Can I transfer or sell my copyright?
body.answer: Yes, copyright can be assigned or licensed for commercial purposes. Professionals Law drafts proper agreements for such transfers.
